title: WendyOS fleet manifest (wendy-fleet.json)
description: A fleet placement/topology manifest kept separate from a project's single-app wendy.json. It describes topology only: each component references an app directory (whose own wendy.json defines the app) and the device tags it deploys to. The Go parser (appconfig.FleetManifest) is the source of truth.

appId.description: Optional fleet-level label; each app carries its own appId.

components.description: The apps that make up the fleet, keyed by a label.

component.description: One app of the fleet: an app directory plus the device tags it deploys to.

path.description: App directory relative to wendy-fleet.json; must contain the app's own wendy.json.

tags.description: Device tags this component deploys to: a device matches when any tag matches its name (glob, e.g. "camera-*"). "central" is just a conventional tag.

discovers.description: Other components whose live endpoints are injected into this component.

componentExpose.description: The network endpoint this component serves, advertised for cross-component discovery.

path.description: Optional URL path the component serves (e.g. "/stream"). The advertised discovery URL is the origin (scheme://host:port); consumers append their own paths.

discoverRef.description: Inject another component's live endpoints into this component.

component.description: Name of the exposing component to discover.

as.description: Environment variable to receive the JSON peer snapshot. WENDY_DISCOVERY_URL (live API) is auto-injected and reserved.
